methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Kunin is a Pre-Seed stage company in Climate & Energy founded by Daniel Rau, having raised $1.75M to date. The Climate & Energy sector includes a universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), with notable peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. However, a formal peer-median approach is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Climate & Energy peer set—only 21 sector peers exist broadly, with none meeting the filtering criteria of ≥3 datapoints in stage-adjacent or similar-valuation tiers.

Consequently, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. Using the $1.75M total raised as a proxy foundation ($2M rounded), a typical Pre-Seed-to-next-stage multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $4M. This single-method triangulation produces a low of $3.7M, median of $4M, and high of $5M.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $4M–$5M, with $4M as the central estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct pre-seed comparables in the sector, which limits confidence in stage-progression assumptions, and the nascent nature of frontier climate technology markets where traditional step-up multiples may not hold. A more robust view would require either closer-stage comparable data or clarity on product maturity and capital efficiency relative to sector peers.
